research_problem.value: The paper addresses the challenge of utilizing high-quality dense rewards at scale for reinforcement learning of large language models (LLMs), particularly in online process reward model (PRM) updates.
research_problem.evidence: We identify the central challenge as how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale, which enables online process reward model (PRM) update efficiently.

current_limitations.value: Existing methods require building complex human annotation pipelines or rely on estimation-based methods, which are not scalable in online RL.
current_limitations.evidence: Existing methods either need to build complicated human annotation pipelines or rely on estimation-based methods, which require about $1 0 \times$ more rollouts for each step than sampling only the response-level trajectories.

key_challenges.value: Three critical challenges are identified: (1) difficulty in defining process rewards, (2) scalability issues in PRM online updates, and (3) extra costs associated with explicit reward modeling.
key_challenges.evidence: C1. Process rewards are hard to define... C2. PRM online updates are not scalable... C3. Explicit reward modeling brings extra cost.

provenance.section_header: 2.2 KEY CHALLENGES IN SCALABLE DENSE REWARDS

main_idea.value: PRIME applies implicit process rewards derived from the Implicit PRM, trained with outcome labels only, enabling online updates to avoid reward hacking.
main_idea.evidence: The key insight of PRIME is to apply implicit process rewards, which are derivable from the Implicit PRM that is trained with only outcome labels.

inspiration_source.value: The theoretical basis for PRIME is rooted in the ability to repurpose an ORM as a PRM at inference, leveraging causal language models.
inspiration_source.evidence: Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

objective.value: The objective of the Implicit PRM is to train dense reward models using outcome-level labels, enabling token-level dense rewards at inference without additional costs.
objective.evidence: Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

method.value: The Implicit PRM uses outcome labels to train, repurposing them as token-level dense rewards at inference.
method.evidence: Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

reward_calculation.value: Rewards are calculated using the formula: r_ϕ(y_t) := β log π_ϕ(y_t|y_<t)/π_ref(y_t|y_<t), where π_ϕ is the RM and π_ref is the reference model.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

training_objective.value: The training objective is to update the Implicit PRM using outcome labels and policy rollouts, ensuring efficient and scalable online updates.
training_objective.evidence: We update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

workflow.value: PRIME initializes policy model and Implicit PRM with reference models, samples multiple responses for each prompt, filters outputs, obtains implicit process rewards, updates PRM using cross-entropy loss, computes advantage and policy loss, then updates the policy model.
workflow.evidence: Figure 2: Illustration of PRIME. PRIME follows that (1) initialize policy model and the Implicit PRM both with the reference model; (2) sample multiple responses for each prompt and filter with output accuracy; (3) obtain implicit process rewards by the Implicit PRM and update it using cross-entropy (CE) loss; (4) compute advantage and policy loss then update the policy model.

key_techniques.value: Key techniques include implicit process reward modeling, scalable reward updates, and integration of token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards.
key_techniques.evidence: PRIME adopts implicit process rewards for efficient PRM online update (C2), then integrates token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards in MC advantage estimate (C1).

inference_enhancement.value: Inference is enhanced by using token-level dense rewards to estimate advantages, improving sample efficiency and reducing reward hacking risks.
inference_enhancement.evidence: In PRIME, upon rollouts being generated and graded by the (ground truth) outcome verifier, we update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision and then calculate token-level dense rewards to estimate advantages.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

experimental_setup.value: Experiments focus on mathematical and coding problems, starting with Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base model, using a dataset of reasoning instructions from open-source datasets, and evaluating on 7 reasoning benchmarks.
experimental_setup.evidence: We focus on mathematical and coding problems in this paper. For models, we start with Qwen2.5- Math-7B-Base... Evaluation Benchmarks. We evaluate on 7 reasoning benchmarks, focusing on competition-level mathematics and programming tasks.

algorithm_effectiveness.value: PRIME achieves 15.1% improvement on average and over 20% on AMC and AIME competitions, surpassing several other models.
algorithm_effectiveness.evidence: Eurus-2-7B-PRIME achieves substantial improvements on key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT version of the model, leading to $1 5 . 1 \%$ improvement on average, and over $20 \%$ on AMC and AIME competitions.

acceleration_effect.value: PRIME leads to 2.5 times sample efficiency gain compared to RL using outcome rewards only.
acceleration_effect.evidence: Compared to RL using outcome rewards only, PRIME achieves a $2 . 5 \times$ sample efficiency gain.

online_prm_importance.value: Online PRM updates are crucial for preventing reward overoptimization and hacking, improving accuracy throughout training.
online_prm_importance.evidence: Online PRM update is essential... The figure clearly shows that, online update mitigates overoptimization and reward hacking.

provenance.section_header: 5.1 DESIGN CHOICES FOR THE IMPLICIT PRM

inference_scaling_effect.value: Dense rewards in PRIME lead to lower variances and consistent outperformance compared to setups using only outcome rewards.
inference_scaling_effect.evidence: Dense rewards in PRIME lead to $2 . 5 \times$ sample efficiency and $6 . 9 \%$ performance improvement. PRIME also substantially outperforms RLOO on downstream tasks.

main_findings.value: PRIME significantly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance, is easy to use with minimum cost, and works with broad RL algorithms.
main_findings.evidence: Throughout the experiments, we validate that PRIME (1) greatly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance, (2) is easy to use with minimum cost, and (3) is a general method that works with broad RL algorithms together.

technical_contributions.value: PRIME introduces implicit process reward modeling, scalable online updates, and integration of token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards, enhancing RL for LLMs.
technical_contributions.evidence: PRIME adopts implicit process rewards for efficient PRM online update (C2), then integrates token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards in MC advantage estimate (C1).

practical_contributions.value: PRIME reduces development overhead by eliminating the dedicated reward modeling stage, improving resource efficiency.
practical_contributions.evidence: PRIME combines well with various advantage functions and forgoes the dedicated reward model training phase that existing approaches require, substantially reducing the development overhead.

community_contributions.value: PRIME's open-source implementation and results on reasoning benchmarks contribute to the community's understanding of RL for LLMs.
